expectations.text: Review identifies SQL injection vulnerability in the login or register endpoint
expectations.evidence: Line 12: '[SECURITY][error] SQL injection via unsanitized username and password in login query' — explicitly calls out f-string interpolation into SQL, provides exploit example, recommends parameterized queries

expectations.text: Review flags MD5 as insecure for password storage and suggests bcrypt/argon2/scrypt
expectations.evidence: Lines 53-73: '[SECURITY][error] MD5 used for password hashing — cryptographically broken' — explains no salt, rainbow tables, suggests bcrypt with code example

expectations.text: Security issues are marked as error severity (not just warnings or info)
expectations.evidence: Both SQL injection and MD5 findings explicitly tagged [SECURITY][error]

expectations.text: Overall verdict is REQUEST CHANGES given error-severity security issues
Review includes a structured summary with category breakdown (table or list)
expectations.evidence: Lines 179-186: Markdown table with Bugs/Security/Performance/Code Quality/Test Coverage rows with Error/Warning/Info columns
